articles.title: Redundant Anchor Systems for Overhung Indoor Walls
articles.summary: Design and inspection strategies for redundant, multidirectional anchors on steep lead walls in modern gyms.
articles.content: This article covers advanced principles for building and inspecting redundant anchor systems on overhung terrain. Topics include bolt spacing on arêtes, direction-of-load analysis, master point equalization techniques, backup strategies for fixed-top-rope anchors, and failure case studies from indoor environments...

articles.title: Load Testing Modern Indoor Anchors
articles.summary: Empirical data on shear and outward force performance for common indoor anchor configurations.
articles.content: Indoor anchor design has evolved alongside wall construction techniques. In this study we instrumented common anchor layouts and recorded forces during leader falls, top-rope loads, and dynamic lowers. We compare stainless glue-ins, mechanical bolts, and resin-bonded hardware, and provide recommendations for factor-of-safety and inspection intervals...

articles.title: Efficient Anchor Cleaning and Transition Techniques for Lead Instructors
articles.summary: Standardized methods for teaching safe, efficient anchor cleaning in crowded indoor settings.
articles.content: Lead instructors must balance safety, clarity, and throughput when teaching anchor cleaning in busy gyms. This article outlines step-by-step transition flows for lower-through and re-thread scenarios, discusses common error patterns in new leaders, and recommends communication scripts and backup systems suitable for group instruction...

articles.title: Indoor Anchor Building Fundamentals
articles.summary: A step-by-step guide to basic indoor anchor construction for new staff and instructors.
articles.content: This foundational guide introduces SERENE principles, common indoor hardware, and simple redundant anchor configurations for top-rope setups...

articles.title: Dynamic Belay Techniques for Indoor Lead Falls
articles.summary: How to coach modern dynamic belay technique without compromising fall clearance.
articles.content: Dynamic belaying is now standard practice in high-end lead gyms. We break down stance, rope handling, and decision-making for advanced belayers, with drills for instructors...

articles.title: Designing a Youth Bouldering Curriculum
articles.summary: Age-appropriate progression ideas for youth bouldering programs.
articles.content: Youth bouldering programs require thoughtful progression to develop movement literacy while minimizing overuse injuries...

articles.title: Building an Effective Gym Emergency Response Plan
articles.summary: From minor injuries to major incidents, every facility needs a rehearsed plan.
articles.content: We outline the components of a written emergency response plan and how to run realistic drills with staff...

articles.title: Designing Routesetting Calendars for High-Volume Gyms
articles.summary: Scheduling strategies that balance freshness, staff capacity, and member expectations.
articles.content: We present sample rotation calendars, communication templates, and KPI ideas for routesetting programs...

articles.title: Member Engagement Strategies for Small Climbing Gyms
articles.summary: Low-budget tactics to keep climbers returning between route sets.
articles.content: From theme nights to feedback loops, small gyms can punch above their weight in member engagement...

articles.title: Feedback Loops That Actually Improve Athlete Performance
articles.summary: Practical frameworks for giving and receiving feedback in climbing coaching relationships.
articles.content: We explore structured debriefs, micro-goal tracking, and video review workflows for teams and private clients...

articles.title: Making Route Grading More Inclusive
articles.summary: How to communicate difficulty in ways that serve diverse bodies and backgrounds.
articles.content: Traditional grading systems can alienate certain climbers. We discuss alternatives, signage, and staff training...

articles.title: Creating a Staff Training Ladder for Belay and Lead Skills
articles.summary: A modular progression for onboarding new instructors and floor staff.
articles.content: We propose a multi-step training ladder with clear competency checklists for belay, lead, and rescue skills...

articles.title: Risk Assessment for Indoor Bouldering Competitions
articles.summary: Identifying and mitigating comp-specific hazards for youth and adult events.
articles.content: Competition settings concentrate risk in time and space. We outline a practical risk assessment checklist...

articles.title: Optimizing Gym Layout for Flow and Supervision
articles.summary: Layout decisions that improve supervision, customer flow, and revenue per square foot.
articles.content: From sightlines to circulation, we review common layout pitfalls and better alternatives for indoor climbing spaces...

committees.name: Indoor Safety Standards – Evening Online Committee
committees.description: This committee maintains and updates indoor climbing safety standards, with a focus on practical implementation in commercial gyms.
committees.purpose: Review incident data, update belay and lead policies, and publish recommended practices for member facilities.
committees.meetingCadence: Monthly on the first Tuesday at 7:00 PM Pacific Time via video conference.
committees.requirements: Open to staff and owners of member gyms with at least 2 years of industry experience.

committees.name: On-Site Safety Audits Working Group
committees.description: Field-focused group coordinating peer safety audits at member facilities.
committees.purpose: Develop and refine the standardized gym inspection checklist and share audit outcomes anonymously.
committees.meetingCadence: Quarterly in-person meetings rotated between major hubs.
committees.requirements: Must hold a current safety or inspection-related certification.

committees.name: Education & Curriculum Committee
committees.description: Oversees curricula for coaching, instructional, and professional development courses.
committees.purpose: Align course offerings with current best practices and create new learning pathways for members.
committees.meetingCadence: Meets every six weeks via video call.
committees.requirements: Preferably holds a national or international coaching or instructor certification.

committees.name: Competitions Rules & Formats Committee
committees.description: Defines standard formats and rulesets for sanctioned indoor competitions.
committees.purpose: Maintain the official rulebook, publish updates, and respond to rule interpretation requests.
committees.meetingCadence: Bi-monthly with one virtual and one in-person summit each year.
committees.requirements: Experience organizing or setting for at least three competitions.

committees.name: Membership Benefits Advisory Council
committees.description: Advises on member services, discounts, and partner programs.
committees.purpose: Ensure membership tiers remain valuable and accessible to a wide range of professionals.
committees.meetingCadence: Every other month via video conference.
committees.requirements: Open to current individual or gym members.

committees.name: Governance & Board Relations Committee
committees.description: Supports the board in governance best practices and policy updates.
committees.purpose: Review bylaws, conflict-of-interest policies, and long-term governance structures.
committees.requirements: Invitation-only; prior nonprofit board experience preferred.

committees.name: Safety Training Guidelines Subcommittee
committees.description: Focuses on staff and instructor training guidelines for belaying, leading, and rescue.
committees.purpose: Publish recommended minimum training standards for front-line gym staff.
committees.meetingCadence: Meets every other month at 6:30 PM local time rotating across time zones.
committees.requirements: At least one year of experience supervising staff training in a climbing facility.

committees.name: Inclusion & Community Advisory Group
committees.description: Advises on inclusion, accessibility, and community outreach initiatives.
committees.purpose: Ensure that association programs support diverse and historically marginalized climbers.
committees.meetingCadence: Monthly 60-minute calls with occasional working sessions.
committees.requirements: Open to all members; lived experience and community involvement valued.

courses.title: Intermediate Lead Coaching Fundamentals
courses.description: A structured program for coaches moving from basic instruction into performance-oriented lead coaching.
courses.syllabus: Week 1: Assessing lead readiness; Week 2: Technique diagnostics; Week 3: Tactical decision-making; Week 4: Fear management; Week 5: Custom training plans; Week 6: Case studies and assessments.

courses.title: Intermediate Team Bouldering Coaching
courses.description: Tools for coaching youth and adult teams in modern competition-style bouldering.
courses.syllabus: Movement themes, session design, comp simulations, and athlete monitoring over eight weeks.

courses.title: Designing Intermediate Training Programs for Climbers
courses.description: Learn to build 4–8 week training cycles for intermediate lead and bouldering athletes.
courses.syllabus: Needs analysis, exercise selection, cycle design, and monitoring with practical templates.

courses.title: High Performance Climbing Coaching
courses.description: Advanced course for coaches working with national-level athletes.
courses.syllabus: Integrated physical, technical, tactical, and psychological preparation.

courses.title: Professional Routesetting Practices
courses.description: Core competencies for full-time routesetters, from logistics to safety and creativity.
courses.syllabus: Safety systems, movement design, workload planning, and communication with stakeholders.

courses.title: Indoor Climbing Safety Manager Certificate
courses.description: Comprehensive program for staff overseeing safety systems and incident response.
courses.syllabus: Policy development, staff training systems, emergency planning, and audits.

courses.title: Coaching Foundations for New Instructors
courses.description: Introductory coaching skills for staff transitioning from floor roles into instruction.
courses.syllabus: Communication basics, lesson planning, and risk management for group sessions.

courses.title: Gym Management Essentials
courses.description: Operational, financial, and HR basics for small to mid-sized climbing gyms.
courses.syllabus: Budgeting, scheduling, membership KPIs, and staff leadership.

courses.title: Head Routesetter Leadership
courses.description: Leadership and communication skills specifically for head routesetters.
courses.syllabus: Team management, feedback culture, and working with ownership.

courses.title: Intermediate Youth Team Management
courses.description: Blend of coaching and administrative skills for youth team head coaches.
courses.syllabus: Parent communication, season planning, travel logistics, and safeguarding policies.

job_postings.title: Head Routesetter – Seattle Flagship Gym
job_postings.description: Lead a team of six full-time routesetters at a high-volume flagship facility. Responsible for creative direction, safety systems, and competition sets.
job_postings.requirements: 5+ years of professional routesetting experience, including 2+ years in a lead or head role. Demonstrated competition setting experience and familiarity with IFSC-style formats.

job_postings.title: Head Routesetter
job_postings.description: Oversee all setting operations for a multi-location gym network in the Portland metro area.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years professional routesetting, strong leadership skills, ability to manage travel between locations.

job_postings.title: Routesetter – Early Career
job_postings.description: Join a growing setting crew supporting youth and adult programming at a Seattle-area bouldering gym.
job_postings.requirements: 1+ year of commercial setting experience and strong movement skills.

job_postings.title: Youth Team Coach
job_postings.description: Coach youth competitive teams from local to regional level at a Denver facility.
job_postings.requirements: 2+ years coaching youth climbers and familiarity with regional competition formats.

job_postings.title: Gym Manager – Urban Bouldering Facility
job_postings.description: Oversee day-to-day operations, staff leadership, and community engagement at a busy urban bouldering-only gym.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years management experience in climbing or related industries, strong communication skills.

job_postings.title: Front Desk & Member Services – Part Time
job_postings.description: Help members and guests with check-in, gear rental, and basic facility questions.
job_postings.requirements: Customer service experience preferred; climbing knowledge a plus.

job_postings.title: Head Routesetter – Remote Consulting (Part-Time)
job_postings.description: Provide remote consulting and periodic on-site visits for a network of small bouldering gyms.
job_postings.requirements: 5+ years of routesetting experience and ability to travel quarterly.

job_postings.title: Routesetter – Bay Area Location
job_postings.description: Help shape the look and feel of a new bouldering gym opening in the Bay Area.
job_postings.requirements: 2+ years commercial setting; comfort with competition-style movement.

job_postings.title: Head Coach – Youth Competition Team
job_postings.description: Lead a multi-squad youth team program, including season design and assistant coach oversight.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years coaching competitive youth teams, strong communication with parents and gym leadership.

job_postings.title: Assistant Gym Manager
job_postings.description: Support the general manager in running daily operations and staff at a Denver-area gym.
job_postings.requirements: Supervisory experience and strong organizational skills.

job_postings.title: Front Desk & Membership Coordinator
job_postings.description: Full-time membership and front desk role focusing on sales and member engagement.
job_postings.requirements: Customer service and sales experience, enthusiasm for climbing.

job_postings.title: Head Routesetter – International Destination Gym
job_postings.description: Take on a high-profile head routesetter position at a destination gym outside North America.
job_postings.requirements: 5+ years of professional routesetting experience and prior head setter role strongly preferred.

membership_plans.name: Professional Routesetter Membership
membership_plans.description: Individual membership designed for professional routesetters, including access to standards, job board boosts, and education discounts.
benefits: Access to professional routesetting guidelines and standards
Visibility badge on job board profile

membership_plans.name: Individual Professional Membership
membership_plans.description: For coaches, gym staff, and other individual professionals seeking access to resources and member pricing.

membership_plans.name: Student Professional Membership
membership_plans.description: Discounted membership for full-time students working or volunteering in climbing gyms.
benefits: Same benefits as Individual Professional Membership at a reduced rate

membership_plans.name: Gym Partner – Basic
membership_plans.description: Entry-level membership for single-location climbing gyms.
benefits: Facility listing in online gym directory
Access to safety standards and sample policies

membership_plans.name: Gym Partner – Premium
membership_plans.description: Enhanced membership for multi-location gyms and regional chains.
benefits: Priority access to consulting and audits

membership_plans.name: Corporate Supporter
membership_plans.description: Membership for brands and vendors supporting the indoor climbing industry.
benefits: Access to industry reports and events

membership_plans.name: Legacy Routesetter Membership
membership_plans.description: Deprecated legacy membership for routesetters, maintained for existing members only.
benefits: Grandfathered benefits for legacy routesetter members

membership_plans.name: Youth Program Provider
membership_plans.description: Membership option for organizations that primarily operate youth climbing programs.
benefits: Access to youth program safety templates
Eligibility to list youth programs in directory
